f1d99d0b.title: Senior Functional Consultant - Oracle Fusion Cloud Order Management - 9841
f1d99d0b.description:
Job Location: Pune
   

  

 Location Flexibility: Multiple Locations in Country
   

  

 Req Id: 9841
   

  

 Posting Start Date: 7/10/26
   

  

 At Fujitsu, our purpose is to make the world more sustainable by building trust in society through innovation. Founded in Japan in 1935, Fujitsu has been a pioneer in technology and innovation for decades. Today, as a world\-leading digital transformation partner, we are committed to transforming business and society in the digital age.
   

  

 With approximately 130,000 employees across over 50 countries, Fujitsu offers a broad range of products, services, and solutions. We collaborate with our customers to co\-create solutions that drive enterprise\-wide digitalization while actively working to address social issues and contribute to the United Nations Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs).
   

  

 Job Role: Senior Functional Consultant \- Oracle Fusion Cloud (Order Management)
   

  

 Shift: UK Shift (2\.00 PM to 11\.00 PM)
   

  

 Work Mode: Hybrid
   

  

 Relevant Exp: 9\+ Years
   

  

**Job Description**
 Role Type: Managed Services / Application Support
   

  

 Primary Modules: Oracle Order Management Cloud, Pricing, Quoting Support, Fulfillment, Oracle CPQ (Preferred, but not required)
   

  

**Role Summary**
 We are seeking an experienced
 **Oracle Order Management** 
 Cloud Support Consultant to provide managed services support for Customer’s Oracle Order Management and related order\-to\-cash application processes. The resource will support day\-to\-day production issues, enhancements, user inquiries, order processing, pricing, fulfillment, and quoting\-related processes.
   

  

 The ideal candidate should have strong hands\-on experience in Oracle Fusion Cloud Order Management, with working knowledge of quote\-to\-order and order\-to\-cash processes. Oracle CPQ experience is a plus, but not mandatory. The resource should be able to support quoting\-related data flow, pricing issues, order creation, orchestration, fulfillment exceptions, integrations, and coordination with business and technical teams.
   

  

**Key Responsibilities**
 Provide ongoing managed services support for Oracle Order Management Cloud and related order\-to\-cash functions.
   

  

**Support Business Users With Issues Related To**
 Quote\-to\-order flow
   

  

 Sales order creation
   

  

 Order capture
   

  

 Pricing
   

  

 Discounts
   

  

 Holds
   

  

 Order orchestration
   

  

 Fulfillment
   

  

 Shipping coordination
   

  

 Returns/RMAs
   

  

 Customer and item\-related order issues
   

  

 Analyze and resolve incidents, service requests, configuration issues, and user inquiries.
   

  

 Troubleshoot order failures, stuck orders, orchestration errors, fulfillment exceptions, pricing errors, and integration issues.
   

  

 Support quoting\-related processes even where Oracle CPQ is not implemented, including quote validation, quote conversion, pricing review, and quote\-to\-sales\-order handoff.
   

  

 Work with upstream systems such as CRM, quoting tools, customer portals, EDI, or custom applications to support quote and order flow.
   

  

 Collaborate with downstream teams supporting Inventory, Shipping, Receivables, Manufacturing, Procurement, and integrations.
   

  

 Review and support configurations related to order orchestration, pricing strategy, fulfillment lines, holds, processing constraints, document sequencing, and approvals.
   

  

 Support issue triage involving REST/SOAP integrations, FBDI imports, scheduled processes, and order import failures.
   

  

 Assist with minor enhancements, configuration changes, testing, and deployment activities.
   

  

 Prepare functional specifications for reports, integrations, extensions, workflows, or process improvements as needed.
   

  

 Participate in Oracle quarterly update impact analysis, regression testing, and production validation.
   

  

 Coordinate with Oracle Support for SR creation, tracking, and resolution where required.
   

  

 Maintain support documentation, knowledge articles, issue logs, and resolution notes.
   

  

 Ensure timely response and resolution in line with agreed SLA expectations.
   

  

**Required Skills And Experience**
 10\+ years of experience in Oracle ERP applications, with at least 3\+ years in Oracle Fusion Cloud Order Management.
   

  

 Strong functional knowledge of Oracle Cloud Order Management processes, including:
   

  

 Order capture
   

  

 Order orchestration
   

  

 Pricing
   

  

 Fulfillment
   

  

 Holds and exceptions
   

  

 Returns/RMA processing
   

  

 Order revisions and cancellations
   

  

 Working knowledge of quote\-to\-order and order\-to\-cash business processes.
   

  

 Experience supporting quoting\-related activities such as quote validation, quote pricing, quote conversion, quote\-to\-order handoff, and order creation from upstream systems.
   

  

 Ability to troubleshoot order import, orchestration, pricing, fulfillment, and integration issues.
   

  

 Experience working in a production support or managed services environment.
   

  

 Understanding of integrations between Oracle Order Management and related applications such as:
   

  

 Inventory Management
   

  

 Shipping
   

  

 Pricing
   

  

 Receivables
   

  

 Manufacturing
   

  

 Procurement
   

  

 Customer Data Management
   

  

 Third\-party quoting or CRM systems
   

  

 Familiarity with Oracle scheduled processes, ESS jobs, error logs, and diagnostic tools.
   

  

 Experience working with Oracle Support and managing Service Requests.
   

  

 Ability to work directly with business stakeholders to analyze issues and recommend practical solutions.
   

  

 Strong written and verbal communication skills.
   

  

**Preferred Qualifications**
 Oracle CPQ experience is preferred but not mandatory.
   

  

 Oracle Cloud Order Management or SCM certification is a plus.
   

  

 Experience supporting manufacturing, distribution, automotive, or industrial products companies is preferred.
   

  

 Knowledge of Oracle Pricing Cloud, including price lists, discount lists, pricing strategies, and pricing algorithms.
   

  

 Familiarity with EDI order flows, order import, customer portals, and third\-party sales channels.
   

  

 Experience with configurable products, configured orders, kits, models, or assemble\-to\-order processes is a plus.
   

  

 Understanding of drop ship, back\-to\-back, make\-to\-order, and internal material transfer flows is beneficial.
   

  

 Knowledge of OTBI, BI Publisher, and order management reporting is a plus.
   

  

**Module / Process\-Specific Expectations**
 Oracle Order Management Cloud
   

  

 Support sales order creation, order revisions, cancellations, holds, approvals, and order status tracking.
   

  

 Troubleshoot order processing errors, stuck orders, orchestration failures, and fulfillment exceptions.
   

  

 Support business users with day\-to\-day order management questions and operational issues.
   

  

 Quoting / Quote\-to\-Order
   

  

 Support quoting\-related business processes and data flow from quote creation through sales order conversion.
   

  

 Analyze quote\-to\-order issues involving pricing, customer data, item configuration, discounts, approvals, and order handoff.
   

  

 Work with upstream quoting, CRM, or custom applications to resolve data and integration issues.
   

  

**Oracle CPQ Experience Is Helpful But Not Required.**
 Pricing
   

  

 Support pricing setup and troubleshooting related to price lists, discount lists, pricing strategies, charges, adjustments, and manual price overrides.
   

  

 Investigate pricing discrepancies between quote, order, invoice, or downstream systems.
   

  

 Fulfillment / Orchestration
   

  

 Support order orchestration, fulfillment line errors, shipping interface issues, and fulfillment delays.
   

  

 Collaborate with Inventory, Shipping, Manufacturing, and Procurement teams to resolve cross\-functional issues.
   

  

 Troubleshoot order\-to\-shipment and order\-to\-invoice process failures.
   

  

 Integrations
   

  

 Support issue triage for integrations involving upstream quote/CRM systems, EDI orders, customer portals, shipping systems, and financial applications.
   

  

 Analyze integration errors, failed imports, rejected orders, and data mapping issues from a functional perspective.
   

  

**Soft Skills**
 Strong analytical and problem\-solving mindset.
   

  

 Ability to work independently in a managed services model.
   

  

 Customer\-focused approach with strong ownership of assigned issues.
   

  

 Ability to communicate effectively with business users, IT teams, offshore teams, and Oracle Support.
   

  

 Strong prioritization skills with ability to manage multiple tickets and support activities simultaneously.
   

  

 Comfortable working in a fast\-paced order fulfillment environment.
   

  

 Ability to document issue resolution steps and contribute to support knowledge base.
   

  

 Relocation Supported: Yes
   

  

 Visa Sponsorship Approved: No
   

  

 At Fujitsu, we are committed to an inclusive recruitment process that values the diverse backgrounds and experiences of all applicants. We believe that hiring people from a wide variety of backgrounds makes us stronger, not because it's the right thing to do, but because it allows us to draw on a wider range of perspectives and life experiences.

bd6d090c.title: Technical Program Manager
bd6d090c.description:
**About the Company** 




 At Redpin we simplify life's most important payments. Buying a new property overseas can be a stressful time, especially when it comes to moving your money. Through our Currencies Direct and TorFX brands we've been helping people do just that for over 25 years. With recent investment we're now on a mission to build a new range of digital products and services that will make moving money internationally for real estate purchases even easier.
 



  





 We're on a mission to become the solution for real estate payments everywhere. To do this, we are transitioning our business from a horizontal FX platform to a verticalized, embedded software company, as we look to the future and Redpin 2\.0\.
 



  





 As part of this mission, we're thrilled to announce we have opened a new office in Hyderabad!
 



  





**About the Role** 




 You will own the delivery of Redpin's most important cross\-functional business initiatives — the programs the company is betting on — and be accountable for the outcome, not just the plan.
 



  





 Success means bringing multiple teams into alignment on scope, dependencies, and timelines, holding that alignment as things change, and driving execution through to shipped. You'll operate across Engineering, Product, Operations, and the CTO's office, turning ambiguous, high\-stakes goals into a plan teams believe in and then landing it. When a program starts slipping from green to yellow to red, you're the one who sees it first and does what it takes to turn it back
 



  





 You'll do this as a nimble, async\-first operator — not by filling calendars with status meetings.
 



  





**What You'll Do** 



* Own end\-to\-end delivery of key cross\-functional initiatives across Core Platform, from planning to ship.
* Drive quarterly planning: shape goals with leadership, decompose initiatives, map dependencies, and align teams on scope and timelines.
* Maintain an honest RAG view of program health; detect risk early, escalate the right issues to the right people, and drive the actions that turn red/yellow back to green.
* Unblock teams — broker decisions, remove dependencies, and make pragmatic calls rather than logging problems and waiting.
* Manage risk and dependencies across teams and third parties (banking partners, payment rails, critical vendors).
* Keep leadership informed through crisp written updates that drive decisions without a meeting.



  





**What You’ll Need** 



* Proven track record delivering complex, cross\-functional technical programs in a fast moving environment.
* Strong technical fluency — enough to earn engineers' trust, reason about architecture and trade\-offs, and make pragmatic decisions.
* Excellent written communication and stakeholder management across ICs, managers, Directors, Product, Operations, and executive leadership; strong persuasion and influence without authority.
* Sharp risk and escalation instincts — you recognise "yellow turning red" early and know how to intervene.
* Comfort with ambiguity and a bias to action; async\-first working style.
* Experience with quarterly/OKR planning and dependency management at scale



  





**Nice to have** 



* Experience in payments, fintech, FX, or banking, and comfort where compliance and delivery intersect.
* Exposure to third\-party vendors, banking partners, or platform integrations.



  





**How we work** 




 Async\-first and pragmatic. We default to writing, meet only when a decision needs a room, and value judgement over process. We're not looking for a TPM who runs programs by the book or blocks progress with ceremony — we're looking for the person who is the reason initiatives ship.

1203ff1f.title: SAP Delivery Manager
1203ff1f.description:
**Your Job**
 The SAP Delivery Manager will be a part of the Business Segments Delivery Team for Koch Industries. Koch Industries is a privately held global organization with over 120,000 employees around the world, with subsidiaries involved in manufacturing, trading, and investments. KGS, India is being developed to extend its IT operations, as well as act as a hub for innovation in the IT function. As KGS rapidly scales up its operations in India, it’s employees will get opportunities to carve out a career path for themselves within the organization. This role will have the opportunity to join on the ground floor and will play a critical part in helping build out the KGS over the next several years. Working closely with global colleagues would provide significant global exposure to the employees. This role is a part of the Georgia\-Pacific team within the KGS. GP is completely owned by Koch Industries.
   

  

**Our Team**
 SAP Delivery Manager will report to the SAP Delivery Leader of the KGS and will be a part of an international team that designs, develops, and delivers new applications for Koch Industries. KGS, India is being developed to extend its IT operations, as well as act as a hub for innovation in the IT function.
   

  

**What You Will Do**
* Lead and develop the KGSI SAP RTR team, fostering a culture of accountability, collaboration, and continuous improvement.
* Coach, mentor, and grow SAP Finance talent through capability development, cross\-skilling, and succession planning.
* Build strong partnerships with Finance stakeholders and serve as a trusted advisor on technology\-enabled business solutions.
* Collaborate with Finance leadership to define priorities, roadmaps, and strategic improvement initiatives.
* Oversee SAP RTR processes including General Ledger, Asset Accounting, financial close, reporting, and related integrations.
* Own delivery of SAP Finance support, enhancements, projects, and transformation initiatives.
* Drive process simplification, automation, and innovation across Finance operations and SAP platforms.
* Champion the adoption of AI, automation, and advanced analytics to improve business outcomes and user productivity.
* Provide functional leadership for SAP Finance solutions with deep expertise in COPA and Product Costing.
* Support profitability, cost management, and financial decision\-making through effective use of COPA and Product Costing capabilities.
* Ensure operational excellence through effective service management, issue resolution, and SLA performance.
* Support period\-end close activities to ensure system stability and business continuity.
* Partner with IT, Data \& Analytics, and business teams to deliver integrated enterprise solutions.
* Ensure compliance with financial controls, audit requirements, and governance standards.
* Provide strategic leadership while maintaining appropriate functional oversight of SAP Finance solutions.


**Who You Are (Basic Qualifications)**
 Leadership \& Business Partnership
   

  

* Proven experience leading SAP Finance/RTR teams within a global delivery model.
* Demonstrated success building strong partnerships with Finance and business stakeholders, translating business needs into technology solutions.
* Strong stakeholder management, communication, and influencing skills across all organizational levels.
* Ability to lead organizational change, drive adoption of new capabilities, and promote a business\-partner mindset within support teams.
* Experience developing and mentoring SAP professionals while fostering a culture of accountability, innovation, and continuous improvement.


 SAP Finance Functional Expertise
   

  

* Strong understanding of end\-to\-end finance processes and integrations
* 10\-14\+ years of SAP Finance experience with expertise across core finance processes.
* Deep expertise in:
* Profitability Analysis (COPA)
* Product Costing
* Experience with AI, automation, and analytics technologies that enhance Finance processes and business outcomes, is highly desirable.


 Delivery \& Operational Excellence
   

  

* Experience managing global production support organizations, including incident management, service delivery, and SLA compliance.
* Proven track record of driving delivery excellence, operational efficiency, and continuous improvement.
* Ability to balance operational support responsibilities with delivery of strategic initiatives and business transformation programs.


**What Will Put You Ahead**
* Experience in S/4HANA transformation programs
* Exposure to organizational design and span of control optimization
* Experience managing large or distributed teams
* Strong focus on talent development and capability building
* Exposure to adjacent SAP modules (MDG, P2P, OTC)
* Experience in automation and digital transformation initiatives
* Prior experience in a player\-coach leadership role


**Who We Are**
* Ability to work in a global delivery model (India–US collaboration)
* Flexibility to support critical financial cycles (month\-end, year\-end)
* Strong ownership mindset and proactive communication
* Ability to balance strategic leadership with operational responsibilities


 At Koch, employees are empowered to do what they do best to make life better. Learn how our business philosophy helps employees unleash their potential while creating value for themselves and the company.
   

  

 Additionally, everyone has individual work and personal needs. We seek to enable the best work environment that helps you and the business work together to produce superior results.

abe32161.title: Engagement Manager ( Immediate Joiners )
abe32161.description:
**About Us** 




  






 Observe.AI
 
 is the enterprise\-grade Customer Experience AI platform that unifies conversations, intelligence, and action to turn contact centers into performance engines. Built to optimize the full lifecycle of human and AI agents,
 
 Observe.AI
 
 enables enterprises to automate customer interactions, augment agent performance, and deliver governed AI at scale.
 



  





 On a single platform,
 
 Observe.AI
 
 combines Voice and Chat AI Agents, real\-time AI Copilots, and Conversation Intelligence with 100% interaction coverage for quality, compliance, and performance management. Trusted by brands like DoorDash, Affordable Care, Signify Health, and Verida,
 
 Observe.AI
 
 delivers fast time\-to\-value, measurable ROI, and consistent, high\-quality customer experiences across every channel.
 



  





**Why join us** 




  





 As an Engagement Manager for AI Agent deployments, you’ll own how we implement VoiceAI and ChatAI for our enterprise customers \- from strategy through execution. Your job is to make every deployment smooth, high\-impact, and set up for long\-term success.
 



 This role blends program leadership, customer strategy, and value realization. You’ll guide complex rollouts, coordinate across multiple teams, manage risks, and ensure every launch delivers clear, measurable outcomes.
 



  





  





**What You’ll be Doing** 




  





**Lead End\-to\-End AI Agent Delivery:** 



* Own AI Agent deployments from kickoff → design → build → testing → go\-live → hypercare → optimization.
* Conduct business and technical discovery to deeply understand customer workflows, KPIs, and success criteria.



**Orchestrate a Cross\-Functional Delivery Pod:** 



* Lead a matrixed delivery team including Experience Designers, AI Agent Engineers, and Telephony Engineers.
* Coordinate with Engineering on feature gaps, technical escalations, and roadmap alignment.



**Drive Customer Outcomes \& Long\-Term Success:** 



* Own the success of multi\-phase AI transformation programs.
* Post–go live, act as the customer’s primary AI Agent program lead \- running QBRs, tracking KPIs, and driving continuous value.



**Establish Scalable Processes \& Governance:** 



* Create and refine playbooks, delivery templates, evaluation frameworks, UAT processes, hypercare models, and best practices
* Ensure compliance with testing, evaluation frameworks, UAT processes, and deployment checklists.
* Manage timelines, deliverables, documentation, and cross\-functional dependencies for multiple concurrent customer programs.



**Customer Enablement \& Training:** 



* Guide customers through operational readiness, change management, and best practices for scaling AI within their contact centers.



**Be the Face of AI Strategy for Enterprise Customers:** 



* Present confidently to frontline leaders, IT executives, and C\-suite stakeholders.
* Translate complex AI/technical concepts into business outcomes and guide customers through AI maturity and transformation journeys.



  





**What You Bring to the Role** 



* 5\+ years in enterprise customer delivery, professional services, program management, or consulting (SaaS, AI, CX, digital transformation, or contact center domains preferred)
* Proven success leading complex enterprise implementations with cross\-functional and executive stakeholders.
* Strong understanding of contact center KPIs like containment, AHT, CSAT, NPS, and how they influence AI Agent projects.
* Comfort leading customer\-facing discussions \- from deep technical troubleshooting to weekly project demos.
* Demonstrated ability to manage multiple projects simultaneously in fast\-paced, evolving environments.
* Excellent communication, training, documentation, and relationship\-building skills.



  





**Nice to have:** 



* Experience with Conversational AI, VoiceAI, RAG systems, or NLU/NLP platforms.
* Hands\-on experience to CCaaS platforms (Genesys, NICE, Amazon Connect, Five9, Talkdesk, Zoom Contact Center



  





**Perks \& Benefits** 



* Excellent medical insurance options and free online doctor consultations
* Yearly privilege and sick leaves as per Karnataka S\&E Act
* Generous holidays (National and Festive) recognition and parental leave policies
* Learning \& Development fund to support your continuous learning journey and professional development
* Fun events to build culture across the organization
* Flexible benefit plans for tax exemptions (i.e. Meal card, PF, etc.)



  





**Our Commitment to Inclusion and Belonging** 




*Observe.AI
 
 is an Equal Employment Opportunity employer that proudly pursues and hires a diverse workforce. Observe AI does not make hiring or employment decisions on the basis of race, color, religion or religious belief, ethnic or national origin, nationality, sex, gender, gender identity, sexual orientation, disability, age, military or veteran status, or any other basis protected by applicable local, state, or federal laws or prohibited by Company policy. Observe.AI also strives for a healthy and safe workplace and strictly prohibits harassment of any kind.* 




  





*We welcome all people. We celebrate diversity of all kinds and are committed to creating an inclusive culture built on a foundation of respect for all individuals. We seek to hire, develop, and retain talented people from all backgrounds. Individuals from non\-traditional backgrounds, historically marginalized or underrepresented groups are strongly encouraged to apply.* 




  





*If you are ambitious, make an impact wherever you go, and you're ready to shape the future of Observe.AI, we encourage you to apply. For more information, visit
 
 www.observe.ai
 
 .*

7208b179.title: Engagement Manager
7208b179.description:
**Join the team shaping the future of enterprise AI.** 








**About Moveworks and 89 Solutions** 




 89 Solutions is a pure play Moveworks partner delivering enterprise\-grade AI solutions that transform employee support and productivity. With a proven track record across the United States and Australia, we lead the market in successful Moveworks implementations and advanced Agent Studio use cases.
 



 Recognised as the world’s most trusted Moveworks partner, we drive measurable business outcomes through precision, reliability, and innovation
 



 Our values are simple:
 



 ✅ Outcome obsessed
 



 ✅ Have fun
 



 ✅ People over profit
 







 The Role
 



 We’re looking for an
 **Engagement Manager** 
 to join our Expert Services team in India, supporting global enterprise customers. This is a hands‑on delivery role that blends project management, consulting, and business analysis\-ideal for someone who thrives on ownership, pace, and impact.
 







 You’ll be in the driver’s seat from discovery through delivery, keeping teams aligned, risks controlled, and outcomes on track across AI and enterprise SaaS engagements.
 











**Key Responsibilities** 




 • Owns and leads client engagements end\-to\-end, from discovery through to go\-live
 



 • Drives use case definition, business process analysis, and requirements gathering
 



 • Manages engagement governance including scope, timelines, risks, dependencies, and reporting
 



 • Acts as the primary point of contact for stakeholders across customer and internal teams
 



 • Partners with technical teams to design and shape AI and conversational solutions across platforms such as
 



 ServiceNow, Salesforce, Microsoft Teams, and Slack
 



 • Maintains a strong delivery cadence through clear communication, demos, and proactive issue management
 



 • Champions quality, consistency, and adoption through structured delivery and effective change management
 







**Qualifications** 



* 5\+ years of experience in consulting or services as an Engagement Manager, Project Manager, or Senior Business Analyst
* Proven delivery experience in complex, fast‑paced enterprise environments
* Exposure to AI, automation, or enterprise SaaS implementations
* Strong stakeholder management, documentation, and communication skills
* Experience working in Agile, Waterfall, or hybrid delivery models
* An ownership‑driven mindset, with the ability to anticipate risks and drive outcomes



  





**Must Have:** 




**Background of BA and PM role with** 




**strong Communication** 




**Problem solving attitude** 




**Risk Management** 




**ServiceNow candidates are preferred** 




  





**Nice to Have** 



* CBAP, PMI‑PBA, CSPO, or similar certifications
* AI or automation training (e.g., Microsoft AI Fundamentals, UiPath, Dialogflow)
* Comfort operating in global delivery models with cross‑regional stakeholders

The ideal candidate will be responsible for managing cross\-functional programs, driving execution, and ensuring successful delivery of strategic initiatives in a fast paced environment.

53e399f1.title: Senior Program Manager
53e399f1.description:
As a
 **Program Manager** 
 , you drive multiple programs reaching beyond your immediate team and organization. You proactively identify, organize, and drive program activities and stakeholders in order to successfully deliver business, operational, and technical improvements. You scope, phase, and drive ambiguous cross\-functional and cross\-organizational challenges including delivering downstream change management, alongside your stakeholder team. You ensure alignment of senior cross\-functional and cross\-organizational stakeholders. You have advanced knowledge of the domain of your program.
 



  





  





**Skills required** 




 :• Strong communication skills to interact with engineering and product teams
 



 .• Candidate must be tech\-savvy and capable of data analysis to provide suggestions
 



 .• Proficiency in technical languages such as SQL or Python is considered a plus
 



  





**.** 




**Responsibilities includ** 




 \-Across multiple teams, independently define, drive, and own program charter(s) that address scope, and timeline, OKRs, resourcing and risks/dependencies of moderately complex programs. Translate ambiguous program goals into clear and measurable objectives/milestones. Determine the effective level of program management required of an initiative. Create project plans that break down a project into discrete phases aligned to the needs of stakeholders, includes: risks/dependencies and support proactive identification of program constraint
 



 \- Independently determine program phasing and metrics that effectively address continuous improvement including Resource Stewardship. Optimize regular checkpoints and metrics dashboards. Ensure stakeholders are aligned on rationale for program pivots.
 



 \- Independently design and deliver optimized program communication plans including executive and key partner communications. Articulately represent the program across touchpoints.
 



 \- Anticipate change management requirements. Independently develop change management rollout plans for programs including impact analysis. Proactively drive pivots requiring change management, monitor progress of change management rollout, and resolve misalignments. Define future change management needs.
 



 \- Effectively build trusted relationships with senior program stakeholders to proactively enable effective decision making. Resolve prioritization misalignments with stakeholders. Proactively manage program escalations.
 



 \- Independently determine and collect data required to govern programs and effectively drive stakeholder decision\-making. Independently maintain project trackers and status reports. Identify and execute on program tasks, including proactively managing dependencies, while incorporating Product Excellence values, Product Inclusion, and brand trust and reputation. Create automated processes to enhance velocity and monitoring. Define program governance structure by determining effective management of mitigations, postmortems, and escalation reports. drive effective program stakeholder meetings.

25fd595f.title: Track Manager - Data security and compliance,Presales
25fd595f.description:
Chennai, Tamil Nadu
  
Job Summary
  
  

This role is responsible for driving pre\-sales activities focused on Governance, Risk, and Compliance (GRC) security solutions, ensuring alignment with client requirements and organizational objectives. The individual leads large\-scale, complex operations, providing expert guidance in security solution design, proposal development, and process optimization, while fostering innovation and empowering teams to exceed client expectations.
  
  

The Data Security Consultant supports the governance and operationalize the Data Protection \& Data Governance program to discover, classify, protect, and remediate sensitive data across the enterprise.
  
  

Key Responsibilities
  
  

1\. Lead the development of security pre\-sales solutions by leveraging Security Pre\-sales and GRC frameworks, ensuring alignment with client requirements and business objectives.
  
2\. Utilize security risk assessment tools and GRC platforms to analyze client environments, identify gaps, and recommend tailored risk mitigation strategies.
  
3\. Oversee the preparation and delivery of security solution proposals, presentations, and RFP responses using industry\-standard security and GRC methodologies.
  
4\. Drive process improvements in pre\-sales operations by implementing best practices in security solution design, workflow automation, and management reporting.
  
5\. Mentor and guide pre\-sales consultants and product management teams in the application of security standards, regulatory compliance requirements, and solution positioning.
  
6\. Engage with internal teams to promote innovation in security pre\-sales offerings, introducing new tools and approaches to enhance service delivery and client satisfaction.
  
7\. Ensure effective communication of project goals, solution benefits, and technical details to both technical and non\-technical stakeholders.
  
  

Build, maintain, and enforce the Data Protection and Data Governance program, including classification, data labeling, the Safe Data program, and personal data transfer processes. • Monitor the organization’s data security posture for protection and exfiltration risk, and enforce global policies and processes. • Define and implement data classification and labeling strategies across repositories (fileshares, cloud storage, databases, SaaS). • Design, deploy, and operate technical controls to protect sensitive data (DLP, endpoint controls, encryption). • Partner with DSPM, DLP, and Cloud teams to design discovery, classification, and remediation workflows for sensitive data. • Develop program\-level metrics and dashboards (coverage, classification rates, incidents, false positive trends). • Drive cross\-functional engagement with Legal, Privacy, JJT, business units, and vendors to ensure compliance and adoption. • Design and deliver training and awareness programs for privileged users, exception users, and business stakeholders. • Create and maintain runbooks, SOPs, test plans, and technical documentation,lead policy and control testing. • Support incident response for data\-related events and lead post\-incident remediation and lessons\-learned activities. • Deliver project plans, timelines, and storyboards for workstreams and operational enhancements; produce step by step guides, FAQs, and training materials. • Produce monthly and quarterly status reports, gather stakeholder feedback, and highlight milestones, risks, issues, and opportunities. • Provide Level 3 (L3\) Data Security consultation and subject\-matter support.( i.e. personal data transfer process)
  
  

Skill Requirements
  
  

1\. Excellent Knowledge Of Security Technologies, Grc Platforms (Such As Archer, Servicenow Grc), And Security Operations Processes.
  
2\. Advanced Skills In Developing And Presenting Security Solution Proposals, Including Technical Documentation And ExecutiveLevel Presentations.
  
3\. Excellent Communication, Stakeholder Management, And Leadership Skills, With The Ability To Drive HighImpact Initiatives And Mentor Teams.
  
4\. Strong Analytical And ProblemSolving Abilities In The Context Of Security And Grc Operations.
  
  

Bachelor’s degree in IT, Cybersecurity, Privacy, Law, or a related field, or equivalent practical experience. • 1–3\+ years of experience in data protection, privacy, or security, including hands\-on work with data transfers and DLP. • Strong analytical abilities with excellent written and verbal communication; proven ability to collaborate effectively across cross\-functional teams. • Solid understanding of data privacy requirements (e.g., GDPR) and regional variations relevant to the role. • Commitment to confidentiality and compliance with non\-disclosure agreements. • Proficiency with data transfer platforms, DLP tools, and collaboration tools (e.g., SharePoint, Power Apps). • Knowledge of configuring and enforcing access restrictions to protect sensitive data.
  
  

Other Requirements
  
  

**Job Title:** Data Security Consultant • Department: Information Security Role Overview The Data Security Consultant supports the governance and operationalize the Data Protection \& Data Governance program to discover, classify, protect, and remediate sensitive data across the enterprise. • Key Responsibilities • Build, maintain, and enforce the Data Protection and Data Governance program, including classification, data labeling, the Safe Data program, and personal data transfer processes. • Monitor the organization’s data security posture for protection and exfiltration risk, and enforce global policies and processes. • Define and implement data classification and labeling strategies across repositories (fileshares, cloud storage, databases, SaaS). • Design, deploy, and operate technical controls to protect sensitive data (DLP, endpoint controls, encryption). • Partner with DSPM, DLP, and Cloud teams to design discovery, classification, and remediation workflows for sensitive data. • Develop program\-level metrics and dashboards (coverage, classification rates, incidents, false positive trends). • Drive cross\-functional engagement with Legal, Privacy, JJT, business units, and vendors to ensure compliance and adoption. • Design and deliver training and awareness programs for privileged users, exception users, and business stakeholders. • Create and maintain runbooks, SOPs, test plans, and technical documentation,lead policy and control testing. • Support incident response for data\-related events and lead post\-incident remediation and lessons\-learned activities. • Deliver project plans, timelines, and storyboards for workstreams and operational enhancements; produce step by step guides, FAQs, and training materials. • Produce monthly and quarterly status reports, gather stakeholder feedback, and highlight milestones, risks, issues, and opportunities. • Provide Level 3 (L3\) Data Security consultation and subject\-matter support.( i.e. personal data transfer process) • Required Qualifications • Bachelor’s degree in IT, Cybersecurity, Privacy, Law, or a related field, or equivalent practical experience. • 1–3\+ years of experience in data protection, privacy, or security, including hands\-on work with data transfers and DLP. • Strong analytical abilities with excellent written and verbal communication; proven ability to collaborate effectively across cross\-functional teams. • Solid understanding of data privacy requirements (e.g., GDPR) and regional variations relevant to the role. • Commitment to confidentiality and compliance with non\-disclosure agreements. • Proficiency with data transfer platforms, DLP tools, and collaboration tools (e.g., SharePoint, Power Apps). • Knowledge of configuring and enforcing access restrictions to protect sensitive data.

9ae447dd.title: Presales Solution Architect
9ae447dd.description:
**Overview**
 The Pre\-sales Solution Architect (EU/US) is the commercial and technical bridge between Prodapt's AI\-native product
   

  

 portfolio and its telecom enterprise customers across the EU and US markets. Part hardcore pre\-sales engineer, part
   

  

 domain authority, this role turns customer pain into winning solution narratives \- grounded in deep telecom functional
   

  

 knowledge (BSS/OSS capabilities, TM Forum processes), technical precision (information models, APIs, network
   

  

 technologies), and a sharp grasp of the business value being unlocked. They are the voice of feasibility in the sales cycle
   

  

 and the voice of the customer back into the product team
   

  

**Responsibilities**
 CORE RESPONSIBILITIES
   

  

* Lead technical discovery with enterprise telecom clients \- map pain points to product capabilities and define the solution


 scope.
   

  

* Architect end\-to\-end solution designs across Prodapt AI\-native platforms, integrating with customer BSS/OSS


 landscapes.
   

  

* Own the response to RFPs, RFIs, and PoC requests; produce solution blueprints, architecture diagrams, and valuecase documents.
* Deliver compelling technical demonstrations and proof\-of\-concepts that translate product capabilities into measurable


 customer outcomes.
   

  

* Articulate business value: build ROI models, TCO analyses, and benefit realization frameworks tailored to each


 customer context.
   

  

* Act as the feedback loop from market and pre\-sales back to Product Architect \- surface unmet needs, competitive gaps,


 and feature requests.
   

  

* Collaborate with delivery and engineering teams to ensure solutions are buildable and transition smoothly from presales


 to execution.
   

  

* Represent Prodapt at industry forums, TM Forum events, and customer advisory boards as a recognized telecom


 domain expert.
   

  

* Engage CSP customers across EU and US regions \- tailor solution narratives to regional market dynamics, operator


 maturity, and buying behaviors; travel to customer sites across both regions as needed.
   

  

**Requirements**
 TELECOM DOMAIN EXPERTISE \- MUST\-HAVE
   

  

* Functional depth across BSS (ordering, billing, product catalogue, CRM) and OSS (inventory, provisioning, assurance,


 network management).
   

  

* Mastery of TM Forum frameworks: eTOM process model, TAM application map, SID information model, and Open API


 suite (TMF620, TMF641, TMF622, TMF632, etc.).
   

  

* Strong understanding of network technologies: 5G core, RAN, IP/MPLS, SD\-WAN, NFV/SDN, and cloud\-native network


 functions (CNFs).
   

  

* Hands\-on experience with telecom information models \- resource, service, and product layer decomposition \- and how


 they map to API contracts.
   

  

 Hiring Profiles \| AI\-Native Product Team \| 2026
   

  

 Prodapt Solutions Pvt. Ltd. \| Confidential Page 2 of 2
   

  

* Familiarity with AI/automation use cases in telecom: AIOps, predictive maintenance, autonomous network operations,


 intelligent order management.
   

  

* Working knowledge of the EU/US operator landscape (tier\-1 and challenger CSPs, MVNOs, wholesale/fiber players)


 and the regional regulatory context \- GDPR, EU AI Act, NIS2, FCC/CPNI \- as it shapes solution design and data
   

  

 handling.
   

  

**Technical Skills**
* Proficient in API design and integration patterns \- REST, GraphQL, AsyncAPI, event\-driven architectures \- within


 telecom ecosystems.
   

  

* Able to read and produce architecture artefacts: solution blueprints, sequence diagrams, data flow diagrams, and


 integration maps.
   

  

* Working knowledge of cloud platforms (AWS / Azure / GCP) and containerized deployment patterns relevant to telco


 workloads.
   

  

* Comfortable evaluating AI/ML platform capabilities and articulating how LLM\-powered or agentic solutions apply to


 telecom operations.
   

  

 MINDSET \& BEHAVIOURS
   

  

* Credibility\-first \- earns equal trust with a CTO or a NOC team lead; speaks the language of both.
* Value\-oriented \- never pitches features; always leads with outcomes and quantified business impact.
* Curiosity\-driven \- stays current on telecom industry shifts (ORAN, network\-as\-a\-service, AI\-RAN) and weaves them into


 solution narratives.
   

  

* Collaborative closer \- works the deal as a team sport alongside sales, product, and delivery; no lone\-wolf behavior.


**Preferred Qualifications**
* B.S. / M.S. in Telecommunications, Computer Science, or Engineering; TM Forum certifications (eTOM / Frameworx


 Practitioner) and TOGAF a strong plus.
   

  

* Prior roles at a telecom operator, network equipment vendor, or systems integrator in a presales or architecture


 capacity.
   

  

* Published thought leadership, TM Forum Catalyst participation, or conference presentations in the telecom/OSS/BSS


 space.
   

  

* Established relationships within EU/US telecom operator accounts and eligibility to work and travel across both regions;


 comfort operating across EU/US time zones

39c7b5b2.title: Pre-sales Product Consulting - Senior Professional
39c7b5b2.description:
Join us as a technical tendering leader driving multi\-system integration solutions that power electrification, digitization, and sustainability. You'll translate customer needs into competitive, optimized proposals—bridging engineering excellence with strategic opportunity development.
   

  

**What will you do?**
* Design and optimize multi\-system integration solutions combining electrical, mechanical, and digital technologies
* Lead technical tendering from requirements capture through bid submission, ensuring compliance and competitiveness
* Define system architecture, interfaces, and engineering budgets that set execution teams up for success
* Collaborate with opportunity leaders and tendering teams to deliver differentiated, tailor\-made solutions


**What are the key skills we are looking for?**
* Customer Orientation — advanced level; translating complex client needs into actionable technical solutions
* Risk Assessment — advanced level; identifying and mitigating technical risks throughout the tendering lifecycle
* System Integration — intermediate level; coordinating multi\-technology bricks into cohesive, optimized solutions
* Technical Solution Design — intermediate level; defining end\-to\-end solutions that balance performance and feasibility
* Quotation Management — intermediate level; supporting competitive bid development with accurate technical inputs
* Influencing Skills — intermediate level; guiding stakeholders toward optimal technical and commercial decisions


**What qualifications will make you successful?**
* Strategic thinker who balances technical rigor with commercial realities
* Strong communicator and collaborator across engineering, sales, and project teams
* Comfortable making decisions independently in ambiguous, fast\-paced environments
* Driven by innovation and the challenge of delivering integrated, high\-impact solutions


**What's in it for me?**
* Lead high\-impact projects that shape the future of integrated energy and digital systems
* Work on complex, cutting\-edge challenges with real\-world sustainability impact
* Collaborative culture that values technical depth and continuous development
* Growth opportunities through mentorship, training, and exposure to diverse technical domains


 Bring your expertise to a team that's building the future—apply today!
   

  

 Rewards designed for you
   

  

 Our Total Rewards is our way of saying: We see you and we value you. It’s more than just pay and benefits—it’s a meaningful investment in you. It is designed to help you perform, grow, feel safe, and elevate your potential. The package helps you care for yourself and your family, plan your future, grow your skills and career, collaborate in an inclusive workplace, and contribute to your community. At Schneider Electric, we’re here for what matters most to you. Discover more at our Career Page.
   

  

* Country\-specific programs and initiatives may be available.


**Looking to make an IMPACT with your career?**
 When you are thinking about joining a new team, culture matters. At Schneider Electric, our values and behaviors are the foundation for creating a great culture to support business success. We believe that our IMPACT values – Inclusion, Mastery, Purpose, Action, Curiosity, Teamwork – starts with us.
   

  

 IMPACT is also your invitation to join Schneider Electric where you can contribute to turning sustainability ambition into actions, no matter what role you play. It is a call to connect your career with the ambition of achieving a more resilient, efficient, and sustainable world.
   

  

 We are looking for IMPACT Makers; exceptional people who turn sustainability ambitions into actions at the intersection of automation, electrification, and digitization. We celebrate IMPACT Makers and believe everyone has the potential to be one.
   

  

 Become an IMPACT Maker with Schneider Electric – apply today!
   

  

 €40 billion global revenue
   

  

 \+9% organic growth
   

  

 150 000\+ employees in 100\+ countries
   

  

 You must submit an online application to be considered for any position with us. This position will be posted until filled.
   

  

*Schneider Electric aspires to be the most inclusive and caring company in the world, by providing equitable opportunities to everyone, everywhere, and ensuring all employees feel uniquely valued and safe to contribute their best. We mirror the diversity of the communities in which we operate, and ‘inclusion’ is one of our core values. We believe our differences make us stronger as a company and as individuals and we are committed to championing inclusivity in everything we do.*
*At Schneider Electric, we uphold the highest standards of ethics and compliance, and we believe that trust is a foundational value. Our Trust Charter is our Code of Conduct and demonstrates our commitment to ethics, safety, sustainability, quality and cybersecurity, underpinning every aspect of our business and our willingness to behave and respond respectfully and in good faith to all our stakeholders. You can find out more about our Trust Charter here*
*Schneider Electric is an Equal Opportunity Employer. It is our policy to provide equal employment and advancement opportunities in the areas of recruiting, hiring, training, transferring, and promoting all qualified individuals regardless of race, religion, color, gender, disability, national origin, ancestry, age, military status, sexual orientation, marital status, or any other legally protected characteristic or conduct.*
